reasoning: The reference price for this question will be the Friday, August 15, 2025 closing price (since Aug 17 is a Sunday). On August 15, evidence shows IPG stock experienced a very sharp single-day spike of about 6.32% along with a 5.91% jump in Omnicom (OMC) stock, despite both stocks previously trending downward over the last month. Since IPG is being acquired by OMC at a fixed exchange ratio of 0.344 shares (expected to close H2 2025), IPG tracks OMC closely. Starting the forecast from an artificially inflated post-spike base price implies a lower probability of the stock moving even higher in the immediate short-term due to mean reversion (hangover effect). Accordingly, the 1-week forecast (Aug 24) is below 50%. As we project further out to Sept 16 and Nov 15, natural positive stock drift offsets the tough base-price baseline, and the probability reverts toward 0.50. The minimal risk of the deal breaking (which would be catastrophic for the stock price) adds a slight negative skew, providing further justification for keeping the long-term estimate slightly under 0.50.
